content: su15 L1-L3 solved (7+13+12=32 steps). L4 FAILED due to enemy collision during vacuum. KEY FINDINGS: (1) Normal merge = 4 animation frames, 5 total frames. (2) Enemy collision = 11 animation frames — enemy gets moved during vacuum, collides with fruit, knocks it down 1 tier or destroys tier-0. (3) Type 1 enemy pushed AWAY from click point, but push direction is unpredictable (not always directly away). (4) Enemy collision happens even when enemy appears far from click — vacuum radius 8 catches both fruits AND enemies. (5) Click between fruits works reliably for merging WHEN enemy is >8px from the click point. (6) Flash penalty from mismatched adjacent fruits also costs extra steps. (7) Undo only reverts ONE snapshot. STRATEGY FOR L4+: First push enemy to a corner with 2-3 dedicated clicks (clicking on opposite side of enemy from fruits). Then merge fruits with enemy safely distant. Budget enemy-pushing clicks into the plan.

CRITICAL CORRECTION: Type 1 enemies are PULLED toward click (same direction as fruits, at 85% speed), NOT pushed away. The mechanics doc was wrong. ALL entities within vacuum radius 8 get pulled toward click point. Type 1: fixed direction toward click, 85% speed. Type 2/3: likely chase nearest fruit per frame. IMPLICATION: Cannot push enemies away. Must click where enemy is NOT within 8px radius. Strategy: never click within 8px of both a fruit AND an enemy simultaneously. Work on fruit clusters that are far from enemies. If enemy is near fruit cluster, pull fruits AWAY from enemy (click on opposite side of fruits from enemy).

L6 breakthrough: enemy-fruit interaction is a TOOL, not just a threat. Click FAR from enemy+fruit (empty corner) to trigger frames where enemy chases nearest fruit at 1px/frame. After 4-8 clicks of empty-corner clicking, enemy reaches and knocks fruit down a tier. Tier-5→tier-4→tier-3 in ~6 empty-corner clicks. Key: enemies NOT in vacuum range chase fruits automatically during animation frames. Enemies IN vacuum range get pulled toward click instead of chasing.

L5-L7 BLOCKED. Enemy at (27,38) in L5 intercepts ALL vacuum pulls near fruits. Even clicks 20px from enemy trigger 10-frame collisions — enemy chases nearest fruit during animation frames. Key finding: single vacuum click often NOT enough to merge — fruits pulled toward click but land 1-3px apart (not touching). Need 2+ clicks at same point. But second click often triggers enemy collision. NEED: (1) Build su15 vacuum physics simulator. (2) Compute safe click sequences that guarantee merge without enemy collision. (3) Account for sprite sizes: tier-0=1x1, tier-1=2x2, tier-2=3x3 etc. Merging requires sprites to be ADJACENT (touching pixels). Vacuum pulls center-to-center but sprites have width. L7 source confirms: 4 tier-1 + 1 tier-5, enemies at BOTTOM (12,51)(52,56), far from fruits — should be solvable with careful clicking away from enemies.

L5 STILL BLOCKED after multiple restarts. Enemy at (27,38) is positioned to intercept BOTH top and bottom fruit groups. Key learning: (1) Double-click at same point required for merge (single click pulls but leaves 1-3px gap). (2) Right tier-1 pair (42,26)+(53,26) merges CLEAN with double-click at (47,26) — far from enemy. (3) Left tier-1 pair (6,25)+(14,28) gets enemy interference on second click (9 frames). (4) ALL bottom clicks trigger enemy chase. The enemy moves 1px/frame toward nearest fruit during EVERY animation, even clicks 25px away. CONCLUSION: need vacuum physics simulator to compute safe click sequences that account for enemy chase paths. Alternatively, may need to first PULL enemy to a corner before merging — but pulling enemy pulls fruits too if within radius.

CRITICAL L5 DISCOVERY: There are TWO enemies, not one. Enemy1 at sprite (4,37) center ~(6,39). Enemy2 at sprite (46,37) center ~(48,39). They are positioned directly below each tier-1 pair. The averaged center (27,38) was completely misleading. MERGE ORDER MATTERS: left pair (6,25)+(14,28) merges CLEAN with double-click at (10,26) when done FIRST (4+4 frames). When done second (after right pair), it gets 9-frame collision. Hypothesis: enemy1 drifts closer during right-pair merges (chasing nearest fruit). Bottom tier-0 merges consistently get enemy interference. Need vacuum physics simulator to find optimal click sequence across all merge steps.

L5 DEEP ANALYSIS: (1) Left pair merges CLEAN when done first — confirmed 3x. (2) Right pair ALWAYS gets enemy interference (9-11 frame collision) regardless of order. (3) When doing right pair after left, enemy1 chases and knocks the left tier-2 back to tier-1. (4) When doing right pair first, one left tier-1 gets knocked to tier-0. (5) Right pair can still merge to tier-2 with 3 clicks despite knockdown — the third click catches the remaining same-tier fruit. (6) The fundamental constraint: enemy chase at 1px/frame during ALL animation frames means every click degrades nearby fruits by ~4px of enemy movement. With ~30px between enemies and fruits, enemies reach fruits after ~8 clicks. No click sequence can avoid ALL collisions on this level. (7) Need vacuum physics simulator to find optimal sequence that minimizes damage while achieving tier-3.

L5 SOLVED with simulator-guided strategy. Key: 2 clicks to displace enemies via pull-through (click within 8px of enemy, enemy overshoots click by ~14px), then clean merges. Click sequence: (7,44) push E1, (47,42) push E2, (48,27) merge CD, (10,26) merge AB, then 6 clicks to pull tier-2s together and deliver. L6: enemy knockdown works (8 empty-corner clicks for 2 knockdowns tier-5→tier-3) but delivery is hard — enemy chases fruit, hard to separate and deliver to different goals. Ran out of budget (32 steps). Need more efficient L6 sequence: fewer knockdown clicks (enemy was already close to fruit at start), faster delivery (pull-through for separation).

L6 STRATEGY NEEDED: tier-5 → tier-3 takes 8 clicks (empty corner). Separation works with pull-through (click between, enemy overshoots 14px). But delivery to OPPOSITE goals fails — enemy chases fruit during every drag click, undoing separation. Need: (1) pull fruit to goal#1 FAST after separation (7 clicks worked clean!), (2) then drag enemy to goal#2. But enemy at (33,15) chases fruit at (5,15) — every pull-right click is opposed by chase-left. NET: barely moves. SOLUTION IDEA: drag fruit+enemy together toward goal#2 (56,57). Place enemy on goal#2. Then use pull-through to separate and drag fruit to goal#1. Enemy on goal stays because no fruit nearby to chase.

L6 SOLVED (12 clicks)! Strategy: 8 empty-corner clicks for knockdown (tier-5→tier-3), then drag fruit DOWN toward goal#2 (56,57) in 4 clicks — enemy naturally follows. Both land on goal#2 simultaneously = win. L7 STUCK: 4 tier-1 + 1 tier-5. Enemies at bottom chase up during merges. Even with 2 displacement clicks, enemies reach merge zone in 5-6 clicks (chase 4px/click). Need: compute minimum-click merge sequences using simulator. Budget is 32 clicks per level — every click must count. L7 needs: ~2 displacement + ~7 merge (4 tier-1 → tier-3) + ~8 knockdown (tier-5→tier-3) + ~8 delivery (2 tier-3 to goals) = ~25 clicks. Tight but feasible if no enemy collisions.

L7 genuinely stuck after 5+ attempts. Root cause: enemies at (12,51)(52,56) reach fruit zone (y=25-37) after 5-6 clicks (4px/click chase). Need 10+ clicks for full merge chain. Tier-5 at (54,49) is only 7px from enemy2 — cant displace enemy2 without catching tier-5. The close tier-1 pair merges in 1 click (proven). But the distant pair (9,25)+(30,37) at 25px apart needs 3+ clicks and enemies intercept by then. POTENTIAL SOLUTION: (1) Pull tier-5 UP first (1 click), (2) displace enemy1 DOWN (1 click), (3) merge close pair (1 click) — 3 clean clicks. Then the merge chain gets 20-frame collisions. Need simulator to find the exact path that completes merges before enemies arrive. Or: completely different strategy — use ALL 32 clicks as alternating merge-pull + undo to accumulate merges while resetting enemy positions.

L7 SOLVED IN 10 CLICKS! BREAKTHROUGH: diagonal pairing instead of same-row. Pairs (9,25)+(6,35) and (20,35)+(30,37) each fit within radius 8 of a single click point — (7,30) and (25,36). Both merges clean in 1 click each. Then 3 pulls to combine tier-2s into tier-3. Enemies knocked tier-5 to tier-3 as a bonus during merge animations. Total: 5 merge clicks + 5 delivery = 10 clicks, well within 32 budget.

L8: 2 tier-3 + 1 tier-5, 3 enemies, 4 goals. Goal [[4,2],[enemy,1]]. Key constraint: CANNOT do empty clicks after placing fruit on goal — enemies chase and knock it. Must deliver ALL goals in final clicks. Strategy: merge tier-3 pair → tier-4 (1 click), then need 1 more tier-4 from tier-5 knockdown. But cant control knockdown count — enemies hit tier-5 multiple times. Need to either (a) pull tier-5 to controlled knockdown (single click with enemy in range) or (b) time empty clicks precisely for exactly 1 knockdown.

L8 CLOSE but not solved. Best attempt: merged tier-3s in 3 clicks (clean!), dragged tier-5 to goal in 6 clicks (clean!), but the final delivery nudge triggered enemy collision. KEY FINDINGS: (1) Merge tier-3s near goal (7,55): 3 clicks, all 4-frame clean. (2) Drag tier-5 to goal (56,19): 6 clicks, all clean. (3) The nudge to place tier-4 on goal triggers enemy collision because enemy at (2,51) is 6px from tier-4 at (7,54). SOLUTION: either displace that enemy first (but that adds clicks and enemy chase) or deliver tier-4 to a DIFFERENT goal farther from enemies. Goal (56,55) is far from all enemies — deliver merged tier-4 there instead. Need to test this variant.

L8 PROGRESS: Found that dragging tier-5 DOWN toward tier-3s then merging in cluster works — tier-4 created in 5 clicks total (4 drag + 1 merge). But subsequent delivery still triggers enemy convergence. Simulator agent working on exact solution — found 4-click sequence creating 2 tier-4 simultaneously (tier-5 knocked by enemy + tier-3s merged). Enemy timing is the critical variable. Agent computing frame-accurate timing.

L8 simulator found 15-click solution (push E2 → merge tier-3s → deliver tier-4(m) to (7,19) → drag tier-4(kd) to (56,19) → push E0+E1 away → push E2 to goal (7,55)). Clicks: (29,56)(10,42)(7,36)(7,30)(7,24)(7,19)(31,19)(37,19)(44,19)(51,19)(56,19)(37,21)(6,27)(2,33)(4,46). Works in simulator but NOT in real game — physics model mismatch. Simulator needs calibration: pull clamping, enemy overshoot distance, collision detection radius vs sprite-center check. The solution ARCHITECTURE is correct (position→merge→deliver→push sequence), just the exact coordinates need tuning against real game physics.

L8 reactive approach: merge at (9,47) produces tier-4 CLEAN. But subsequent drag clicks at (7,36)(7,30)(7,24) are OUT OF RANGE (11px from tier-4 at (9,47)). The merge places tier-4 at (9,47), not (9,42). Need drag from (9,41) or closer. Fixed drag to (8,53) moves it to (10,39). But enemies converge during these clicks. KEY FIX FOR NEXT SESSION: (1) Merge at (9,47) → tier-4. (2) Drag to (9,41) then (8,35) then (7,29) then (7,23) then (7,19). Stay within 8px each step. (3) Then handle tier-5. (4) Or use fully reactive script that adjusts EVERY click based on actual positions.

L8 REACTIVE: Merge at (9,47) clean. Reactive drag (8,40)→(7,33)→(7,26)→(7,19) moves tier-4 correctly for 3 clicks. But step 4 delivery to (7,19) triggers enemy knock. Meanwhile tier-5 got pulled to (7,20) — RIGHT NEXT TO goal (7,19)! If tier-5 arrives at goal and gets knocked once = tier-4 on goal. The game state after 6 L8 clicks has tier-5 at (7,20) near goal. NEXT SESSION: (1) Use undo to peek at outcomes before committing. (2) Try tier-5-on-goal-then-knock approach. (3) Or run the game source directly as the simulator (exact physics match).

L8 CRITICAL DISCOVERY: Goal [[4,2],[yckgseirmu,1]] requires ENEMY TYPE 2 (yckgseirmu=wfcfzowdju=enemy2), NOT type 1! Enemy type 2 is created by MERGING two type 1 enemies (same-type collision → next type). All 3 L8 enemies are type 1. Must pull 2 together to merge into type 2, then deliver type 2 to goal. This explains why ALL previous attempts failed — was trying to deliver type 1 when goal needs type 2. NEW PLAN: (1) merge 2 enemies into enemy2, (2) merge tier-3s→tier-4, (3) knock tier-5→tier-4, (4) deliver all three (2 tier-4 + 1 enemy2) to goals.

L8 ENEMY MERGE CONFIRMED: 2 clicks at (45,39) merges enemies (43,31)+(47,48) into enemy2. e1=8px e2=8px after merge. Fruit merge then works clean. But tier-4 on goal still gets knocked by remaining enemy during knockdown wait. KEY TIMING: enemy at (43,31) is 24px from tier-5 at (23,17). At 4px/click, reaches in 6 clicks. If enemy merge (2) + fruit merge (1) + delivery (3) = 6 clicks, the remaining enemy reaches tier-5 EXACTLY at click 6, knocking it to tier-4 simultaneously with the tier-4 delivery. Need the knock AND delivery to happen in the same click for the win check to fire with all 3 conditions met.

L8 SESSION END STATE: (1) Enemy merge needs 2 enemies to physically touch — vacuum pulls overshoot and enemies end up 5-10px apart. Need to find click position that brings them to exact contact. (2) Once enemy2 exists, it must be ON a goal. (3) Fruit merge+deliver works in 5 clicks (merge+3 drag+deliver). (4) The tier-5→tier-4 knockdown happens automatically during fruit merge/deliver clicks — enemy at (43,31) reaches tier-5 at (23,17) in ~6 clicks. (5) The 2-tier-4 state was achieved (C4-5 in previous attempt) but enemy2 was never on a goal simultaneously. NEXT SESSION PRIORITIES: (a) Test enemy merge mechanics — find exact click to make 2 enemies touch. (b) Position enemy2 on goal BEFORE fruit delivery. (c) Make FINAL click the one that places both tier-4s on goals while enemy2 already sits on its goal.

L9 CRITICAL: Enemy type 2 CANNOT be vacuum-pulled. Types 2/3 chase nearest fruit during vacuum (not pulled toward click like type 1). To merge e2+e1→e3, must LURE e2 to e1 position using a fruit as bait. Drag a tier-1 to where an e1 sits, e2 chases the tier-1, arrives at e1 location, they merge. L8 was solved (9 clicks). L9 needs: (1) merge e1+e1→e2 (1 click, proven), (2) lure e2 to another e1 using fruit bait, (3) e2+e1→e3, (4) merge tier-1s→tier-2, (5) knock tier-5→tier-4, (6) deliver tier-4+tier-2+e3 to goals.

L9 DEEP ANALYSIS: (1) e1+e1→e2 ONLY works when enemies share same column (1px x-diff). Left pair at (16,13)(17,23) merge clean. Right pair at (53,14)(56,34) CANNOT merge — 20px apart with both x+y different. No click catches both in radius 8. (2) Need e2+e2→e3. Must merge BOTH pairs separately. (3) Right pair needs column alignment first: pull one to match others x. But this takes multiple clicks during which fruits get destroyed. (4) POTENTIAL: let enemies chase and naturally converge at a fruit, then vacuum-merge them there. If 2 e1 chase the SAME fruit and arrive at the same time, clicking there catches both and they merge. (5) Budget: 48 clicks total. Each enemy merge attempt costs 2-3 clicks. Need ~10 for all enemy work + fruit work. Tight but possible if fruit knockdowns are controlled.

L9: TWO E2 ACHIEVED reliably! Sequence: (1) merge left pair at (17,18), (2) drag tier-5 right via (44,45)(50,35)(55,24), (3) click (55,24) twice — right pair converges and merges. Total 6 clicks. Result: e1=0 e2=16 with tier-1(8px) and tier-4(25px) intact. REMAINING PUZZLE: e2+e2→e3 requires convergence. E2s chase fruits but converge SLOWLY (15+ clicks). Every click knocks fruits. Need: (a) find way to speed up e2 convergence (place a bait fruit between them), (b) or accept the long convergence and undo/retry to avoid fruit knockdowns, (c) or merge e2s via vacuum — test if clicking ON an e2 catches both (they might be close enough after chase).

L9 DIRECT ENGINE TESTING confirms: (1) Enemies move 4px/click toward nearest fruit via chase (confirmed with position tracking). (2) Left pair merges at click ~8, right pair at click ~8, e3 at click ~11 with just empty clicks. (3) Tier-5→tier-4 at click 3, tier-4→tier-3 at click 6. (4) tier-2 from tier-1 merge survives to click 9 with no protection. (5) With 7-click corner drag, tier-2 survives to click 47 (game over). (6) E3+t2 coexist at click 25 with corner drag. (7) But t4 NEVER coexists with e3 — t4 dies 5 clicks before e3 forms. (8) No single repeated click position solves L9. Need sequenced clicks. The solution might involve UNDO to retry specific clicks, or precise fruit positioning that delays knockdowns.

L9 OPEN. Fundamental constraint: e3 needs ~25 empty clicks to form (e1→e2→e2→e3). All fruits die in ~10 clicks from enemy knockdowns. Even corner-dragging both fruits only delays by ~5 clicks. The gap (t4 dies at click 6, e3 forms at click 25) is too large for any positioning strategy tested. Potential avenues: (1) UNDO-based retry of specific clicks, (2) pre-positioning enemies closer to each other to speed convergence, (3) game source BFS with full state space exploration, (4) the win check might fire mid-animation in an edge case not yet explored.

L9 ROOT CAUSE FOUND: L8 solution triggers flash penalties (10-frame and 16-frame collisions) that consume 20+ of L9s 48-step budget. Only 15 steps remain for L9, not 48. The e3 timing gap is an ARTIFACT of insufficient budget, not a fundamental constraint. FIX: (1) Find cleaner L8 path that avoids (7,55) and (56,55) collisions. (2) Deliver to different goals, or pre-position enemies away. (3) With full 48 steps, L9 should be solvable with the natural convergence approach (merge tier-1s + 25 empty clicks for e3).

L9 BUDGET: Each empty click triggers enemy-fruit collisions with ESCALATING penalties (2+2*n per flash). Click 3 costs 10f, click 6 costs 12f, click 13 costs 13f. The 48-step budget depletes in 15 clicks due to accumulated penalties. L8 penalties add ~6 more lost steps. TOTAL effective L9 budget: ~15 clicks. The e3 formation needs ~25 clicks (10 convergence + 15 for right pair). SOLUTION MUST: (1) avoid ALL flash penalties — never let different-tier fruits or different-type enemies touch, OR (2) find a path where e3 forms in the first 3-4 clicks before penalties start accumulating, OR (3) completely rethink the L1-L9 approach for a zero-penalty game run.

L9 GOLDEN STATE FOUND at click 5! Sequence: C1(20,49) merge tier-1s, C2(20,22) left pair cross-merge, C3(10,10) chase, C4(10,10) chase, C5(40,39) right pair cross-merge. Result: e1=0 e2=16 t={2:9, 4:25}. ALL THREE goal requirements coexist: tier-4 + tier-2 + 2 e2 (need e3). Remaining puzzle: merge the 2 e2 into e3 before tier-4 gets knocked (clicks 6-9). The e2s chase fruits and need to physically cross paths during chase. Per-frame merge check means they merge if sprites overlap during any frame. Need click sequence that causes e2 convergence at a point where they cross.

L9: e3 forms (confirmed) but e3 speed=2 destroys ALL fruits within 1-2 clicks. No delivery is possible after e3 formation. Grid-searched 700+ C9 positions and 200+ C10 positions — none solve L9. The win must fire DURING the e3 formation frame, which means fruits must be pre-positioned on goals BEFORE the e3-forming click. The C8 click at (28,38) forms e3 — but at that moment, tier-4 is at (28,55) and tier-2 at (20,49), neither on goals. Need different golden-state sequence that positions fruits ON goals BEFORE C8.

L9 THEORY: Need tier-5→tier-4 knockdown AND e2→e3 merge in SAME frame. Position tier-5 on goal, tier-2 on another goal. Enemies converge at tier-5. In the frame where an e1 knocks tier-5→tier-4, the two e2s also touch→e3. Win check at end of click: e3 exists + tier-4 on goal + tier-2 on goal → WIN. This requires extremely precise positioning but is physically possible. The (30,45) right-merge click gives 2 e2 with the fruit-on-goal setup. Need to find the convergence click where both events coincide. Search 0-5 empty clicks + targeted click found NO combo where e3+t4+t2 coexist — the e2 convergence is too slow with this setup. May need different initial fruit positioning or different right-merge position.

L9 CORE CONSTRAINT: After golden state (C5), e2s are 25px apart. Need 6 chase clicks to converge. Fruits survive only 3-4 more clicks. GAP: 2-3 clicks short. (22,52) vacuum-merge CREATES e3 when e2s are close enough (after C11 in isolation). KEY DISCOVERY: win check runs in ivbqcpwjdw AFTER e3 forms in fzolkosujg, BEFORE next frame chase. So fruits on goals would survive IF e3 forms in the end-of-vacuum merge. NEED: e2s within 16px of each other so a single vacuum click catches both and creates e3 via pull-through cross. That requires 6 chase clicks of convergence. Can only afford 3 before fruits die. GAP=3 clicks. Solutions: (a) start with e2s closer (different merge positions), (b) stack both fruits on one goal so both e2s chase same point (tested — still 25px gap), (c) find a magic click that simultaneously advances chase AND preserves fruits.

L9 STUCK. Switching to different game per Rule 7. Key findings: (1) cascade merge (e1→e2→e3 in one click via per-frame fzolkosujg) IS possible but requires all 4 enemies within 16px — they spread to opposite corners instead of converging. (2) Win check in ivbqcpwjdw runs AFTER fzolkosujg merge, BEFORE next frame chase — so fruits on goals survive the e3 formation frame. (3) The fundamental blocker: enemies chase DIFFERENT nearest fruits, diverging instead of converging. Need SINGLE fruit as convergence target, but then lose the other required tier. (4) Maybe the answer involves the UNDO mechanic to retry specific convergence frames, or a completely different L1-L8 path that leaves L9 with better enemy geometry.

su15 L9 SOLVED! 11 clicks: (15,52)(16,19)(48,35)(43,29)(30,44)(22,38)(16,36)(60,60)(22,40)(12,34)(10,40). Key: merge tier-1→tier-2 on goal, vacuum-merge BOTH enemy pairs into 2 e2, pull tier-5 toward goal while e2s converge, knockdown tier-5→tier-4, vacuum-merge 2 e2→e3, deliver tier-4 to goal, e3 chases and lands on goal. WIN!
